title: Bridge Essay: Beyond the 'second brain': the gut microbiota as a constitutive co-constructor of embodied cognitive network.
concept: 4E cognition — embodied, enactive, extended, embedded mind.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, enactive, extended, embedded mind.

**Beyond the 'second brain': the gut microbiota as a constitutive co-constructor of embodied cognitive network.** - Front Neurosci (2026). DOI: 10.3389/fnins.2026.1808839. PMID: 42318200. Traditional cognitive science has historically confined the mind within the cranium. While the "second brain" metaphor underscores the autonomy of the enteric nervous system, it remains entrenched in a neurocentric paradigm. Here, we propose a transformative framework: the gut microbiota may function as a constitutively relevant contributor to specific embodied cognitive architectures. We contend that cognition, emotion, and behavior are not fully understandable in brain-isolated terms. Instead,
